fixing error that broke all the categories

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name =Mihata Kofun Cluster | native_name = 美旗古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akaizuka Kofun burial chamber | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mihata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|43|N|136|08|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nabari, Mie]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mihata Kofun Cluster'''|美旗古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Mihata neighborhood of the city of [[Nabari, Mie]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular burial mound with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[Mihata Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+ Mihata Kofun Cluster (main tumuli) |- ! Kofun name!! Japanese !! Type !! Size !! Remarks |- |'''Umazuka Kofun'''||馬塚古墳||''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' ||length 142m; 98m dia x 14 m high / 100m width x 6m high ||largest in the cluster |- |'''Kijinzuka Kofun'''||貴人塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 55m; 35m dia x 4.5 m high / 35m width x 4m high ||early 6th century |- |'''Bishamonzuka Kofun'''||毘沙門塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 65m; 44m dia x 7m high / 20m width x 3m high || |- |'''Jōrōzuka Kofun'''||女良塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 100m; 73m dia x 9m high / 40m width x 3m high || |- |'''Akaizuka Kofun'''||赤井塚古墳||''enpun'' ||22m dia x 8.5m high(formerly 30 m dia) ||late 6th century |- |'''Tonozuka Kofun'''||殿塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 92m; 56m dia x 7.2m high / 40m width x 6.2m high ||late 4th century |}
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Umazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Umazuka Kofun File:Kijinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kijinzuka Kofun File:Bishamonzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Bishamonzuka Kofun File:Jorouzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Jōrōzuka Kofun File:Akaizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Akaizuka Kofun File:Tonozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Tonozuka Kofun File:Kozuka Kofun (Nabari), funkyu.jpg|Kozuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:|Mihata Kofun Group}} *[https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=731035&pageCur=8 Mie Prefecture Department of Education] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nabari.lg.jp/s061/060/070/050/01mihatakofungun.pdf Nabari City official report] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Mihata Kofun Cluster | native_name = 美旗古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akaizuka Kofun burial chamber | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mihata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|43|N|136|08|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nabari, Mie]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mihata Kofun Cluster'''|美旗古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Mihata neighborhood of the city of [[Nabari, Mie]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular burial mound with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[Mihata Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Mihata Kofun Cluster | native_name = 美旗古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akaizuka Kofun burial chamber | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mihata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|43|N|136|08|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nabari, Mie]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mihata Kofun Cluster'''|美旗古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Mihata neighborhood of the city of [[Nabari, Mie]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular burial mound with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[Mihata Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+ Mihata Kofun Cluster (main tumuli) |- ! Kofun name!! Japanese !! Type !! Size !! Remarks |- |'''Umazuka Kofun'''||馬塚古墳||''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' ||length 142m; 98m dia x 14 m high / 100m width x 6m high ||largest in the cluster |- |'''Kijinzuka Kofun'''||貴人塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 55m; 35m dia x 4.5 m high / 35m width x 4m high ||early 6th century |- |'''Bishamonzuka Kofun'''||毘沙門塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 65m; 44m dia x 7m high / 20m width x 3m high || |- |'''Jōrōzuka Kofun'''||女良塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 100m; 73m dia x 9m high / 40m width x 3m high || |- |'''Akaizuka Kofun'''||赤井塚古墳||''enpun'' ||22m dia x 8.5m high(formerly 30 m dia) ||late 6th century |- |'''Tonozuka Kofun'''||殿塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 92m; 56m dia x 7.2m high / 40m width x 6.2m high ||late 4th century |}

A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular burial mound with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[Mihata Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Mihata Kofun Cluster | native_name = 美旗古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akaizuka Kofun burial chamber | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mihata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|43|N|136|08|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nabari, Mie]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mihata Kofun Cluster'''|美旗古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Mihata neighborhood of the city of [[Nabari, Mie]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular burial mound with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[Mihata Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name =Mihata Kofun Cluster | native_name = 美旗古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akaizuka Kofun burial chamber | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mihata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|43|N|136|08|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nabari, Mie]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mihata Kofun Cluster'''|美旗古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Mihata neighborhood of the city of [[Nabari, Mie]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular burial mound with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[Mihata Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+ Mihata Kofun Cluster (main tumuli) |- ! Kofun name!! Japanese !! Type !! Size !! Remarks |- |'''Umazuka Kofun'''||馬塚古墳||''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' ||length 142m; 98m dia x 14 m high / 100m width x 6m high ||largest in the cluster |- |'''Kijinzuka Kofun'''||貴人塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 55m; 35m dia x 4.5 m high / 35m width x 4m high ||early 6th century |- |'''Bishamonzuka Kofun'''||毘沙門塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 65m; 44m dia x 7m high / 20m width x 3m high || |- |'''Jōrōzuka Kofun'''||女良塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 100m; 73m dia x 9m high / 40m width x 3m high || |- |'''Akaizuka Kofun'''||赤井塚古墳||''enpun'' ||22m dia x 8.5m high(formerly 30 m dia) ||late 6th century |- |'''Tonozuka Kofun'''||殿塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 92m; 56m dia x 7.2m high / 40m width x 6.2m high ||late 4th century |}
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Umazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Umazuka Kofun File:Kijinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kijinzuka Kofun File:Bishamonzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Bishamonzuka Kofun File:Jorouzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Jōrōzuka Kofun File:Akaizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Akaizuka Kofun File:Tonozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Tonozuka Kofun File:Kozuka Kofun (Nabari), funkyu.jpg|Kozuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)]]

Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. {{Infobox ancient site | name =Mihata Kofun Cluster | native_name = 美旗古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akaizuka Kofun burial chamber | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mihata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|43|N|136|08|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nabari, Mie]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mihata Kofun Cluster'''|美旗古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Mihata neighborhood of the city of [[Nabari, Mie]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular burial mound with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[Mihata Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+ Mihata Kofun Cluster (main tumuli) |- ! Kofun name!! Japanese !! type !! Size !! Remarks |- |'''Umazuka Kofun'''||馬塚古墳||''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' ||length 142m; 98m dia x 14 m high / 100m width x 6m high ||largest in the cluster |- |'''Kijinzuka Kofun'''||貴人塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 55m; 35m dia x 4.5 m high / 35m width x 4m high ||early 6th century |- |'''Bishamonzuka Kofun'''||毘沙門塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 65m; 44m dia x 7m high / 20m width x 3m high || |- |'''Jōrōzuka Kofun'''||女良塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 100m; 73m dia x 9m high / 40m width x 3m high || |- |'''Akaizuka Kofun'''||赤井塚古墳||''enpun'' ||22m dia x 8.5m high(formerly 30 m dia) ||late 6th century |- |'''Tonozuka Kofun'''||殿塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 92m; 56m dia x 7.2m high / 40m width x 6.2m high ||late 4th century |}
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Umazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Umazuka Kofun File:Kijinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kijinzuka Kofun File:Bishamonzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Bishamonzuka Kofun File:Jorouzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Jōrōzuka Kofun File:Akaizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Akaizuka Kofun File:Tonozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Tonozuka Kofun File:Kozuka Kofun (Nabari), funkyu.jpg|Kozuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Mihata Kofun Cluster | native_name = 美旗古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akaizuka Kofun burial chamber | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mihata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|43|N|136|08|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nabari, Mie]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mihata Kofun Cluster'''|美旗古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Mihata neighborhood of the city of [[Nabari, Mie]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular burial mound with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[Mihata Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+ Mihata Kofun Cluster (main tumuli) |- ! Kofun name!! Japanese !! type !! Size !! Remarks |- |'''Umazuka Kofun'''||馬塚古墳||''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' ||length 142m; 98m dia x 14 m high / 100m width x 6m high ||largest in the cluster |- |'''Kijinzuka Kofun'''||貴人塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 55m; 35m dia x 4.5 m high / 35m width x 4m high ||early 6th century |- |'''Bishamonzuka Kofun'''||毘沙門塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 65m; 44m dia x 7m high / 20m width x 3m high || |- |'''Jōrōzuka Kofun'''||女良塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 100m; 73m dia x 9m high / 40m width x 3m high || |- |'''Akaizuka Kofun'''||赤井塚古墳||''enpun'' ||22m dia x 8.5m high(formerly 30 m dia) ||late 6th century |- |'''Tonozuka Kofun'''||殿塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 92m; 56m dia x 7.2m high / 40m width x 6.2m high ||late 4th century |}

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Mihata Kofun Cluster | native_name = 美旗古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akaizuka Kofun burial chamber | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mihata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|43|N|136|08|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nabari, Mie]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mihata Kofun Cluster'''|美旗古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Mihata neighborhood of the city of [[Nabari, Mie]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular burial mound with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[Mihata Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+ Mihata Kofun Cluster (main tumuli) |- ! Kofun name!! Japanese !! type !! Size !! Remarks |- |'''Umazuka Kofun'''||馬塚古墳||''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' ||length 142m; 98m dia x 14 m high / 100m width x 6m high ||largest in the cluster |- |'''Kijinzuka Kofun'''||貴人塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 55m; 35m dia x 4.5 m high / 35m width x 4m high ||early 6th century |- |'''Bishamonzuka Kofun'''||毘沙門塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 65m; 44m dia x 7m high / 20m width x 3m high || |- |'''Jōrōzuka Kofun'''||女良塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 100m; 73m dia x 9m high / 40m width x 3m high || |- |'''Akaizuka Kofun'''||赤井塚古墳||''enpun'' ||22m dia x 8.5m high(formerly 30 m dia) ||late 6th century |- |'''Tonozuka Kofun'''||殿塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 92m; 56m dia x 7.2m high / 40m width x 6.2m high ||late 4th century |}

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Mihata Kofun Cluster | native_name = 美旗古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akaizuka Kofun burial chamber | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mihata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|43|N|136|08|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nabari, Mie]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mihata Kofun Cluster'''|美旗古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Mihata neighborhood of the city of [[Nabari, Mie]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular burial mound with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[Mihata Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+ Mihata Kofun Cluster (main tumuli) |- ! Kofun name!! Japanese !! type !! Size !! Remarks |- |'''Umazuka Kofun'''||馬塚古墳||''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' ||length 142m; 98m dia x 14 m high / 100m width x 6m high ||largest in the cluster |- |'''Kijinzuka Kofun'''||貴人塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 55m; 35m dia x 4.5 m high / 35m width x 4m high ||early 6th century |- |'''Bishamonzuka Kofun'''||毘沙門塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 65m; 44m dia x 7m high / 20m width x 3m high || |- |'''Jōrōzuka Kofun'''||女良塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 100m; 73m dia x 9m high / 40m width x 3m high || |- |'''Akaizuka Kofun'''||赤井塚古墳||''enpun'' ||22m dia x 8.5m high(formerly 30 m dia) ||late 6th century |- |'''Tonozuka Kofun'''||殿塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 92m; 56m dia x 7.2m high / 40m width x 6.2m high ||late 4th century |}

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Mihata Kofun Cluster | native_name = 美旗古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akaizuka Kofun burial chamber | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mihata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|39|43|N|136|08|04|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nabari, Mie]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mihata Kofun Cluster'''|美旗古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mihata neighborhood of the city of [[Nabari, Mie]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular Kofun with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[Mihata Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+ Mihata Kofun Cluster (main tumuli) |- ! Kofun name!! Japanese !! type !! Size !! Remarks |- |'''Umazuka Kofun'''||馬塚古墳||''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' ||length 142m; 98m dia x 14 m high / 100m width x 6m high ||largest in the cluster |- |'''Kijinzuka Kofun'''||貴人塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 55m; 35m dia x 4.5 m high / 35m width x 4m high ||early 6th century |- |'''Bishamonzuka Kofun'''||毘沙門塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 65m; 44m dia x 7m high / 20m width x 3m high || |- |'''Jōrōzuka Kofun'''||女良塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 100m; 73m dia x 9m high / 40m width x 3m high || |- |'''Akaizuka Kofun'''||赤井塚古墳||''enpun'' ||22m dia x 8.5m high(formerly 30 m dia) ||late 6th century |- |'''Tonozuka Kofun'''||殿塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 92m; 56m dia x 7.2m high / 40m width x 6.2m high ||late 4th century |}

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular Kofun with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[Mihata Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+ Mihata Kofun Cluster (main tumuli) |- ! Kofun name!! Japanese !! type !! Size !! Remarks |- |'''Umazuka Kofun'''||馬塚古墳||''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' ||length 142m; 98m dia x 14 m high / 100m width x 6m high ||largest in the cluster |- |'''Kijinzuka Kofun'''||貴人塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 55m; 35m dia x 4.5 m high / 35m width x 4m high ||early 6th century |- |'''Bishamonzuka Kofun'''||毘沙門塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 65m; 44m dia x 7m high / 20m width x 3m high || |- |'''Jōrōzuka Kofun'''||女良塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 100m; 73m dia x 9m high / 40m width x 3m high || |- |'''Akaizuka Kofun'''||赤井塚古墳||''enpun'' ||22m dia x 8.5m high(formerly 30 m dia) ||late 6th century |- |'''Tonozuka Kofun'''||殿塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 92m; 56m dia x 7.2m high / 40m width x 6.2m high ||late 4th century |}

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[:en:Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[:en:Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular Kofun with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mihata Station|Mihata Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mihata Kofun Group]][[en:Mihata Kofun Cluster]][[ja:美旗古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11609095]] Wikidata name: Mihata Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Mihata Kofun Cluster | native_name = 美旗古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akaizuka Kofun burial chamber | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mihata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|39|43|N|136|08|04|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.662028|N|136.134611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nabari, Mie|Nabari, Mie]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mihata Kofun Cluster'''|美旗古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mihata neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nabari, Mie|Nabari, Mie]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[:en:Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[:en:Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular Kofun with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mihata Station|Mihata Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+ Mihata Kofun Cluster (main tumuli) |- ! Kofun name!! Japanese !! type !! Size !! Remarks |- |'''Umazuka Kofun'''||馬塚古墳||''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' ||length 142m; 98m dia x 14 m high / 100m width x 6m high ||largest in the cluster |- |'''Kijinzuka Kofun'''||貴人塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 55m; 35m dia x 4.5 m high / 35m width x 4m high ||early 6th century |- |'''Bishamonzuka Kofun'''||毘沙門塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 65m; 44m dia x 7m high / 20m width x 3m high || |- |'''Jōrōzuka Kofun'''||女良塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 100m; 73m dia x 9m high / 40m width x 3m high || |- |'''Akaizuka Kofun'''||赤井塚古墳||''enpun'' ||22m dia x 8.5m high(formerly 30 m dia) ||late 6th century |- |'''Tonozuka Kofun'''||殿塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 92m; 56m dia x 7.2m high / 40m width x 6.2m high ||late 4th century |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mihata Kofun Group]][[en:Mihata Kofun Cluster]][[ja:美旗古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11609095]] Wikidata name: Mihata Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Mihata Kofun Cluster | native_name = 美旗古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akaizuka Kofun burial chamber | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mihata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|39|43|N|136|08|04|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.662028|N|136.134611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nabari, Mie|Nabari, Mie]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mihata Kofun Cluster'''|美旗古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mihata neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nabari, Mie|Nabari, Mie]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[:en:Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[:en:Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular Kofun with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mihata Station|Mihata Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+ Mihata Kofun Cluster (main tumuli) |- ! Kofun name!! Japanese !! type !! Size !! Remarks |- |'''Umazuka Kofun'''||馬塚古墳||''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' ||length 142m; 98m dia x 14 m high / 100m width x 6m high ||largest in the cluster |- |'''Kijinzuka Kofun'''||貴人塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 55m; 35m dia x 4.5 m high / 35m width x 4m high ||early 6th century |- |'''Bishamonzuka Kofun'''||毘沙門塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 65m; 44m dia x 7m high / 20m width x 3m high || |- |'''Jōrōzuka Kofun'''||女良塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 100m; 73m dia x 9m high / 40m width x 3m high || |- |'''Akaizuka Kofun'''||赤井塚古墳||''enpun'' ||22m dia x 8.5m high(formerly 30 m dia) ||late 6th century |- |'''Tonozuka Kofun'''||殿塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 92m; 56m dia x 7.2m high / 40m width x 6.2m high ||late 4th century |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mihata Kofun Group]][[en:Mihata Kofun Cluster]][[ja:美旗古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11609095]] Wikidata name: Mihata Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Mihata Kofun Cluster | native_name = 美旗古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akaizuka Kofun burial chamber | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mihata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|39|43|N|136|08|04|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.662028|N|136.134611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nabari, Mie|Nabari, Mie]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mihata Kofun Cluster'''|美旗古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mihata neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nabari, Mie|Nabari, Mie]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[:en:Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[:en:Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular Kofun with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mihata Station|Mihata Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+ Mihata Kofun Cluster (main tumuli) |- ! Kofun name!! Japanese !! type !! Size !! Remarks |- |'''Umazuka Kofun'''||馬塚古墳||''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' ||length 142m; 98m dia x 14 m high / 100m width x 6m high ||largest in the cluster |- |'''Kijinzuka Kofun'''||貴人塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 55m; 35m dia x 4.5 m high / 35m width x 4m high ||early 6th century |- |'''Bishamonzuka Kofun'''||毘沙門塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 65m; 44m dia x 7m high / 20m width x 3m high || |- |'''Jōrōzuka Kofun'''||女良塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 100m; 73m dia x 9m high / 40m width x 3m high || |- |'''Akaizuka Kofun'''||赤井塚古墳||''enpun'' ||22m dia x 8.5m high(formerly 30 m dia) ||late 6th century |- |'''Tonozuka Kofun'''||殿塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 92m; 56m dia x 7.2m high / 40m width x 6.2m high ||late 4th century |}
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Umazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Umazuka Kofun File:Kijinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Kijinzuka Kofun File:Bishamonzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Bishamonzuka Kofun File:Jorouzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Jōrōzuka Kofun File:Akaizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Akaizuka Kofun File:Tonozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Tonozuka Kofun File:Kozuka Kofun (Nabari), funkyu.jpg|Kozuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mihata Kofun Group]][[en:Mihata Kofun Cluster]][[ja:美旗古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11609095]] Wikidata name: Mihata Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Mihata Kofun Cluster | native_name = 美旗古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akaizuka Kofun burial chamber | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mihata Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|39|43|N|136|08|04|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.662028|N|136.134611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nabari, Mie|Nabari, Mie]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mihata Kofun Cluster'''|美旗古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mihata neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nabari, Mie|Nabari, Mie]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/216709|title=美旗古墳群|trans-title= Mihata Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest group of ''kofun'' in the [[:en:Iga Province|Iga region]].
==Overview== The Mihata Kofun Cluster is located in the Nabari Basin, and currently consists of seven tumuli. Five of these are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. One is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with a horizontal stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], and one is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}). The oldest dates from the latter half of the 5th century AD and it is speculated that these were the graves of the pre-Yamato conquest chieftains of the [[:en:Iga Province|Iga region]]. There were once more tumuli scattered around this vicinity, but the others have been lost to urban development. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The 88 meter-long Tonozuka Kofun on the northeastern plateau edge is the oldest of the tumuli, from which fragments of horse fittings have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Approximately 300 meters to the southwest are the 100 meter Jōrōzuka Kofun and the Bishamonzuka Kofun, both of which have moats. Approximately 800 meters south of Bishamonzuka Kofun is the Umazuka Kofun which is the largest in the group with a total length of 142 meters. It has a moat 7 to 25 meters wide. A short distance to the east is the square Kozuka Kofun, and 600 meters southeast is the 55-meter long Kijinzuka Kofun, which has a shallow moat with a width of about 6 meters, and which is thought to date from the beginning of the 6th century. On the right bank of the Obata River, which is about 1.5 kilometers south of the Kijinzuka Kofun, is the Akaizuka Kofun, a circular Kofun with a diameter of about 30 meters. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Umazuka Kofun is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Mihata Station|Mihata Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Remarks |- |'''Umazuka Kofun'''||馬塚古墳||''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]'' ||length 142m; 98m dia x 14 m high / 100m width x 6m high ||largest in the cluster |- |'''Kijinzuka Kofun'''||貴人塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 55m; 35m dia x 4.5 m high / 35m width x 4m high ||early 6th century |- |'''Bishamonzuka Kofun'''||毘沙門塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 65m; 44m dia x 7m high / 20m width x 3m high || |- |'''Jōrōzuka Kofun'''||女良塚古墳||''hotategaigata-kofun'' ||length 100m; 73m dia x 9m high / 40m width x 3m high || |- |'''Akaizuka Kofun'''||赤井塚古墳||''enpun'' ||22m dia x 8.5m high(formerly 30 m dia) ||late 6th century |- |'''Tonozuka Kofun'''||殿塚古墳||''zenpō-kōen-fun'' ||length 92m; 56m dia x 7.2m high / 40m width x 6.2m high ||late 4th century |}
